channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Musk calls for annual firing of judges after blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk called for the annual firing of judges following an early Saturday decision from a judge stating that the Treasury Department should bar access to its payment systems to anyone besides “civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hawaii is the rainbow capital of the world. Here's what that means
item.description: Hawaii’s sunshine, short rain showers and clean air together create some of the planet's best conditions for viewing rainbows.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mcavoy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: President Donald Trump’s order declaring diversity, equity and inclusion policies illegal has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: Olga R. Rodriguez, Terry Chea And Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says US doesn't have 'very good security' amid DOGE access concerns
item.description: President Trump on Friday lamented how the U.S. does not have "very good security" when discussing how Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) gained access to sensitive federal information this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Musk asks X if he should rehire DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk took to his social media platform X to ask users if he should rehire one of his deputies who resigned Thursday after racist social media posts of his resurfaced.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Vance tapped to spearhead TikTok deal
item.description: Vice President Vance has been tapped alongside national security adviser Mike Waltz to oversee a potential sale of the popular app TikTok, a source familiar with the plans told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: LG recalls 500K electric ovens after fires, pet deaths
item.description: According to the recall, the front-mounted knobs on the recalled ranges can be accidentally activated by humans or pets.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, NewsNation

item.title: The number of venomous snakes removed from this Australian yard will make you shudder
item.description: A man described feeling “the shudders” as more than 100 venomous red-bellied black snakes were removed from a pile of mulch in his Sydney backyard.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Malicious package found in the Go ecosystem – update
item.description: Update February 6: Google has removed the package and provided the following statement: The module has been removed from both the Go module proxy and GitHub, and we’ve added it to the Go vulnerability database for anyone who thinks they may have been impacted.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google opens door to AI-powered weapons, surveillance
item.description: The company that once opened its code of conduct with the words “Don’t be evil” is now opening the door to assisting in the creation of AI-powered weapons and surveillance systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to bring Google’s custom vibration brilliance to any Android phone today
item.description: Have you heard the buzz? Good vibrations are on their way to Android — or at least to Google’s own homemade Pixel devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success
item.description: Louis Koplin, the acting program executive officer for the Navy’s PEO-Digital, said priorities for 2025 include cyber resiliency and expanding BYOD. The post Navy PEO Digital’s goals riding on Flank Speed’s success first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Quantum networking advances on Earth and in space
item.description: Cisco’s Quantum Summit brought together quantum computing experts last week from around academia, government, and industry and highlighted the progress we’ve seen over the past few months.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, Fox 5
